com.mentor.chs.plugin.harnessimport

Interface IXSignalNameGenerator

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXPlugin
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXSignalNameConfiguration

    public interface IXSignalNameGenerator
extends IXPlugin
    Plugin that generates signal names for imported harness wires. There are 2 phases to the use of a plugin, configuration and generation;

    CONFIGURATION

    Configuration is optional and all configuration methods are defined in the IXSignalNameConfiguration interface. It is expected that if a plugin needs to allow the user to configure it, the plugin should implement the IXSignalNameConfiguration interface.

    GENERATION

    Once the user has pressed the "import" button on the import dialog and the plugin has indicated that it is ready, the harness is imported and signals are generated. The startHarness(com.mentor.chs.api.IXHarness, com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXOutputPrinter) method is called with the imported harness.

    The generated signals are now processed in order and for each signal the getSignalName(com.mentor.chs.api.IXHarness, java.util.Collection<? extends com.mentor.chs.api.IXAbstractConductor>, com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXOutputPrinter) method will be called. The returned name will be applied to the signal object, if valid, and the next signal processed.

    Once all signals have been processed the endHarness(com.mentor.chs.api.IXHarness, com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXOutputPrinter) method will be called to allow the plugin to clean up, deleting any cached information.

    NOTE: Once all signals have been named, if the plugin has generated the same name for two or more signals, the signals will be merged. This will happen even if the associated wires are disconnected.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void endHarness(IXHarness importedHarness, IXOutputPrinter output)
      Called once all signals have been processed.
      java.lang.String getSignalName(IXHarness importedHarness, java.util.Collection<? extends IXAbstractConductor> abstractConductors, IXOutputPrinter output)
      Called once for each identified signal, a signal may have multiple conductors associated with it.
      void startHarness(IXHarness importedHarness, IXOutputPrinter output)
      Called once the harness has been imported, to allow the plugin to perform any harness wide initialization.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • startHarness

        void startHarness(IXHarness importedHarness,
                  IXOutputPrinter output)
        Called once the harness has been imported, to allow the plugin to perform any harness wide initialization.
        Parameters:
        importedHarness - Imported harness that contains wires and signals
        output - to be used by the plugin to report problems.

      • getSignalName

        java.lang.String getSignalName(IXHarness importedHarness,
                               java.util.Collection<? extends IXAbstractConductor> abstractConductors,
                               IXOutputPrinter output)
        Called once for each identified signal, a signal may have multiple conductors associated with it. The returned name will be applied to the signal object by the framework if valid. If the name is not valid it will be discarded and default name used, invalid names include ones that are too long, zero or contain illegal characters.

        Initially, signals are generated by identifying wires that meet at splices. All wires attached to a splice are considered to be in the same signal. If wires do not meet at a splice, they are considered to be in separate signals (this is true even if they meet at a connector cavity). However, a plugin is able to ‘merge’ signals by returning the same name for them.

        Parameters:
        importedHarness - Imported harness
        abstractConductors - Conductors, both shield and wires which will carry the signal to name.
        output - to be used by the plugin to report problems.

      • endHarness

        void endHarness(IXHarness importedHarness,
                IXOutputPrinter output)
        Called once all signals have been processed.
        Parameters:
        importedHarness - Imported harness
        output - to be used by the plugin to report problems.
